channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: DeSantis signs sweeping immigration laws for Florida as states rush to fulfill Trump's agenda
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis signed a sweeping package of immigration laws on Thursday aimed at carrying out President Donald Trump ’s mass deportation agenda.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, STEPHANY MATAT, KIM CHANDLER and CHARLOTTE KRAMON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man injured alongside Salman Rushdie in 2022 stabbing attack says he thought it was a prank at first
item.description: MAYVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — A man injured alongside author Salman Rushdie in a knife attack on a New York lecture stage said Thursday that he tried to stop the assault once he realized it wasn't a prank and was left with a gash above his eye.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Alaska US senators introduce bill that would again designate North America's tallest peak as Denali
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — Alaska's Republican U.S. senators have introduced legislation seeking to designate North America’s tallest peak as Denali — weeks after President Donald Trump issued an executive order calling for the name to revert to Mount McKinley. U.S. Sen.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: AI-powered fitness revolutionizing workouts with adaptive equipment
item.description: “I’m a tech nerd really at heart, and it’s cool to be on that cutting edge,” said Trevor Gere, co-owner of The Smart Fit Method in Northwest Suburban Kildeer.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Barnes, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s buyout offer accepted by about 77,000 federal workers, White House says
item.description: The number amounts to less than 4% of the federal workforce, short of the White House's goal of 5% to 10%.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, KRON4

item.title: What's an atmospheric river? A pineapple express? AP explains the weather phenomenon
item.description: Atmospheric rivers are a plume of moisture that extend across the Pacific to near Hawaii. When they hit land they can cause heavy rains and snow.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NIH reportedly loses two top leaders in two days
item.description: Two key staff members at the National Institutes of Health (NIH) are reportedly leaving the agency amid ongoing efforts by the Trump administration and Department of Government Efficiency head Elon Musk to drastically cut down the size of the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Modi's visit with Trump could boost US-India relations amid tariff woes
item.description: Modi is a nationalist and has talked up his warm relationship with Trump during his first term while cheering his winning back the White House.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Man dies after Las Vegas fire crews find him stuck in palm tree
item.description: A caller reported to Las Vegas Fire and Rescue that they saw an unconscious man about 25 feet in the air and stuck in a palm tree.
item.dc:creator: Julia Romero, NewsNation

item.title: US aircraft carrier collides with merchant vessel in Mediterranean Sea
item.description: The collision occurred between the Nimitz-class aircraft carrier USS Harry S. Truman (CVN 75) and the merchant vessel Besiktas-M, according to U.S. Navy spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Trump signs a plan for reciprocal tariffs on US trading partners, ushering in economic uncertainty
item.description: A trade expert claims that Trump's current tariff plan “reflects a fundamental misunderstanding of how the global economy works.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, KRON4

item.title: Could Kamala Harris be the next California governor? Here's how she's polling
item.description: Kamala Harris holds a substantial lead over other potential candidates to succeed California Gov. Gavin Newsom in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: Senate confirms RFK Jr. as Health secretary; McConnell lone GOP dissenter
item.description: Longtime vaccine critic Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is now the nation’s top health official, after the Senate on Thursday voted almost entirely on party lines to confirm him atop a department of nearly 100,000 employees that runs 13 agencies.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, ABC 4

item.title: Google Calendar users no longer see default entries for events like Pride, Black History Month
item.description: Google's online calendar has removed default references for a handful of holidays and cultural events — with users noticing that mentions of Pride and Black History Month, as well as other observances, no longer appear in their desktop and mobile applications.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jury hands Megan Boswell life sentence after guilty verdict
item.description: SULLIVAN COUNTY, Tenn. (WJHL) — Jurors have returned a guilty verdict against Megan Boswell and sentenced her to life in prison with the possibility for parole after a week-long trial.
item.dc:creator: Murry Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Hurricane Helene victims reunited with dog's ashes
item.description: The Youngs want their story to give other victims of Helene something they may not feel right now: hope.
item.dc:creator: Belle Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Kamala Harris would be Democratic frontrunner for California governor in 2026, poll suggests
item.description: Former Vice President Harris would be the Democratic frontrunner for governor of California if she jumped into the Golden State’s 2026 race to replace Gov. Gavin Newsom (D), new polling shows.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, KRON4

item.title: Trump’s move to slash research funding shakes medical community
item.description: Efforts by the Trump administration to drastically cut federal investments in medical research are threatening to kneecap major research institutions and stifle scientific progress to combat chronic illnesses.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KRON4

item.title: At least 28 are hurt as driver plows into a demonstration in Germany
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A driver drove a car into a union demonstration in central Munich on Thursday, injuring at least 28 people including children, authorities said. Authorities said it was believed to be an attack, and the suspect — an Afghan asylum-seeker — was arrested.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: President Trump's picks face confirmation battles today and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Idaho suspect Bryan Kohberger's bloody new defense claim could fall apart under simple theory, profiler says
item.description: Criminal profiler says Idaho murders suspect could have planted samples from unidentified males whose blood police discovered at King Road crime scene.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Colorado school district first in country to sue Trump administration over fears of ICE raids on campuses
item.description: The Denver Public Schools system has sued the Trump administration for rescinding its policy restricting ICE raids from taking place on school grounds.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Heavy rain pummels slide-prone Southern California as crashes close roads in the Northwest
item.description: Heavy rain is falling across Southern California, creating deluges of water and mud on roads at the bottom of hillsides left barren by recent wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Ben Finley And John Raby, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: O'Reilly predicts Trump and Putin will 'get a ceasefire' in Ukraine
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy "has no power" in opposing a ceasefire deal, according to O'Reilly.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation

item.title: Early look: The views and venues of the 2026 Winter Olympics
item.description: The 2026 Winter Games will bring the world's best athletes to Northern Italy, with Milan and Cortina as key host cities, marking the first time in 20 years that the games return to Italy.
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Orange, Fox 5

item.title: New York isn't alone when it comes to granting driver's licenses to immigrants without legal status
item.description: While U.S. Attorney General Pam Bondi announced legal action Wednesday against New York officials over a 2019 state law allowing immigrants to obtain state-issued driver's licenses regardless of legal status, the Empire State isn't alone in having such a mandate on the books.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN HAIGH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge removes key legal hurdle for Trump’s plan to trim federal workforce with deferred resignations
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Wednesday removed a key legal hurdle stalling President Donald Trump ’s plan to downsize the federal workforce with a deferred resignation program.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, CHRIS MEGERIAN and MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Navy crew members eject from their jet before it crashes off the San Diego coast
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Two crew members ejected from a Navy jet before it crashed Wednesday off the San Diego coast and were quickly rescued by a sportfishing boat, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING and TARA COPP, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Video shows the moment a passenger train smashes into an SUV, driver escapes with seconds to spare
item.description: A Utah Transit Authority train crashed into an SUV on Feb. 4, 2025, causing the vehicle to spin into a pole and completely destroy the front end.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California congressman presents ‘D—- Pic’ during subcommittee hearing
item.description: U.S. Rep. Robert Garcia of Long Beach took aim at billionaire Elon Musk’s role as head of the new Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) during a subcommittee hearing Wednesday morning. Garcia, a Democrat, referenced committee chair Rep.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: John Legend says family is considering leaving California after wildfires
item.description: Last month's devastating wildfires may prompt John Legend and his wife, Chrissy Teigen, the latest celebrities to flee California.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KRON4

item.title: Dog seen riding in unsecured kennel along Indiana expressway now recovering
item.description: Video showed the dog in a kennel on a trailer that was being towed along an Indiana expressway in frigid temperatures.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, KRON4

item.title: Baltimore sues Glock over handguns that can easily be converted to automatic weapons
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Baltimore is suing gunmaker Glock over design features that allow its handguns to be easily modified to become fully automatic weapons, which enables criminals to inflict more damage and makes the city more dangerous, attorneys argue in a complaint filed Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Coca-Cola ICE raids? Company faces backlash in face of unfounded rumors
item.description: (KRON) -- A significant backlash has emerged against soft drink manufacturer Coca-Cola in the wake of rumors that the company laid off its own immigrant employees and then called the U.S. Immigration and Customs and Enforcement Agency on them. Rumors have circulated on X, Reddit, and Facebook.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: When does a heartbeat start? South Carolina Supreme Court again takes up abortion issue
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — With a heartbeat abortion ban solidly in place in South Carolina, lawyers for the state and Planned Parenthood return to the state's highest court Wednesday to argue how restrictive the ban should be.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer in fatal collision at Arizona airport
item.description: One person was killed and others were injured when a private jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer Vince Neil collided with another jet Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Private jets collide at Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, killing 1 person, authorities say
item.description: SCOTTSDALE, Ariz. (AP) — One person was killed and others were injured when a private jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer Vince Neil collided with another jet Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, Fox 5
